items.headline: Bascom Avenue traffic signal project gets third contract amendment
items.summary: The city council approved a third amendment to the consultant agreement for the Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project with DKS Associates. This suggests ongoing work to improve traffic and safety at the rail crossing on Bascom Avenue.

items.headline: City awards contract for street pavement removal and replacement
items.summary: Council approved bids and awarded a contract for the 2026 Local Streets Remove and Replace Pavement Project (item 10626). This work will repair deteriorating streets across the city.

items.headline: City updates Active Transportation Plan with new scoring criteria
items.summary: The City Council reviewed Phase 2 of the Active Transportation Plan, including revised scoring criteria and plan edits that will guide future biking and pedestrian infrastructure projects. This plan shapes how Cupertino prioritizes walking and cycling improvements across the city.

items.headline: Council votes to reduce size of vacant council seats
items.summary: The City Council introduced an ordinance to amend the Council Vacancy Ordinance, changing how vacant positions are filled. This could affect how future council openings are handled and who gets appointed to represent residents.

items.headline: 65-unit senior housing complex approved on East St John
items.summary: Trillium is constructing a nearly $8.8M senior apartment building with 65 units, addressing San José's growing need for affordable housing for older residents. This is the largest housing project in the permit batch and represents significant new housing stock for seniors.

items.headline: Garden Gate Coalition sues city over Mary Avenue safety
items.summary: The City Council discussed ongoing litigation brought by the Garden Gate Coalition for Mary Avenue Safety against Cupertino in Santa Clara Superior Court (Case No. 26CV488611). This case involves disputes over street safety measures on Mary Avenue.

items.headline: Gas utility rates adjusted for fiscal year 2027
items.summary: Palo Alto is modifying residential and commercial gas service rates for the next fiscal year. The rate changes affect both individual households and larger commercial customers across the city.

items.headline: City launches business amnesty program and shop-local campaigns
items.summary: Cupertino introduced a Business License Amnesty Program targeting shopping centers and launched economic development initiatives including the Summer of Celebrations Shop Local Campaign and incentives for retail, small businesses, and startups. These programs aim to support local commerce and bring businesses into compliance.
